an8.85:1.0.1: "Bhikkhus, (1) ‘Ascetic’ is a designation for the Tathāgata, the Arahant, the Perfectly Enlightened One. (2) ‘Brahmin’ is a designation for the Tathāgata, the Arahant, the Perfectly Enlightened One. (3) ‘Healer’ … (4) ‘Master of knowledge’… (5) ‘One unstained’ … (6) ‘Stainless one’ … (7) ‘Knower’ … (8) ‘Liberated one’ is a designation for the Tathāgata, the Arahant, the Perfectly Enlightened One."
an8.85:1.0.2: That highest state to be attained by an ascetic,by a brahmin who has lived the spiritual life,to be attained by a master of knowledge and a healer—that highest state to be attained by one unstained,
an8.85:1.0.3: by a stainless one who is purified,to be attained by a knower, by one liberated—over thatI am triumphant in battle;freed, I free others from bondage.I am a nāga, supremely tamed,one beyond training, attained to nibbāna.
